meaning: Gulf of卡奔塔利亚湾(靠澳大利亚东北部)
examples: An intermittent river of northeast Australia flowing about 837 km (520 mi) northwest to the Gulf of Carpentaria.
The storm hovered between northern Australia and southern New Guinea, covering the Gulf of Carpentaria and much of the Arafura Sea.
From their colony at Java they sent out a small vessel, the Duyfhen, or Dove, which sailed into the Gulf of Carpentaria, and passed half-way down along its eastern side.
Wildlife rangers in the Gulf of Carpentaria say they will mount a search for the crocodile and try to remove it from the wild.
To do that, the ocean-trotter hitched a ride on a current within the Gulf of Carpentaria (that separates Cape York from Arnhem Land, to the west).
